title: A Girl's Skin Problem and the Mystery Behind It
summary: Bethany is a young woman who had eczema, a common skin condition, since she was a child. Doctors gave her steroid creams to help, and they worked for many years. But when she turned 18, her skin got much worse, becoming red, itchy, and painful all over her body. She felt very sick and couldn't do normal things like getting out of bed. Doctors didn't believe her and kept saying it was just eczema, but Bethany knew it was different. She and others share their stories online using the hashtag TSW, which stands for topical steroid withdrawal. This condition is not well understood, and some doctors don't know how to treat it. A professor named Sara Brown is studying TSW to learn more about why it happens and help people like Bethany.

keywords.explanation: a common skin condition that makes skin itchy and red
medicines put on skin to reduce swelling and itching
short for topical steroid withdrawal, a skin problem some people get after using steroid creams
people who help us when we are sick
when scientists research something to learn more about it

questions.question: What skin condition did Bethany have as a child?
What did doctors give Bethany to help her skin?
How did Bethany's skin change when she turned 18?
Who is studying TSW to learn more about it?
Why did Bethany feel upset with doctors?

background_read: Eczema is a skin problem that many people have, making skin dry, itchy, and red. Doctors often use steroid creams to treat it, which are special lotions that reduce swelling. Sometimes, after using these creams for a long time, people might develop a new problem called topical steroid withdrawal (TSW), where their skin gets very bad. This is rare and not fully understood, so scientists are studying it to help patients feel better.
Article_Structure: WHO: Bethany, a young woman with eczema, and Professor Sara Brown, a dermatologist studying TSW.
WHAT: Bethany's skin got worse after using steroid creams, leading to a condition called TSW, which is being researched.
WHERE: In the UK, where Bethany lives and where the study is happening.
WHY: To understand why some people get TSW and to help those suffering from it.

perspectives.description: Bethany and others feel their pain is not believed by doctors and share stories online to raise awareness.
Doctors and researchers are trying to study TSW to diagnose and treat it better, as it is not well understood.
